entries.title:
(c) Excepted position shall have the
same meaning ...
entries.content:
(c) Excepted position shall have the
same meaning as “unclassified posi-
tion”, or “position excepted by law”,
or “position excepted by executive
order”, or “position excepted by Civil
Service Rule”, or “position outside the
competitive service” as used in exist-
ing statutes and Executive orders.

entries.title:
PART 2—APPOINTMENT THROUGH
THE COMPETITIVE SERVICE...
entries.content:
PART 2—APPOINTMENT THROUGH
THE COMPETITIVE SERVICE; RE-
LATED MATTERS (RULE I!)

entries.title:
2.1 Competitive examinations and eligible
register...
entries.content:
2.1 Competitive examinations and eligible
registers; suitability and fitness for civil
service employment.

entries.title: 2.2. Appointments.
entries.content: 2.2. Appointments.

entries.title: 2.3. Apportionment.
entries.content: 2.3. Apportionment.

entries.title: 2.4 Probationary period.
entries.content: 2.4 Probationary period.

entries.title: AUTHORITY: 5 U.S.C. 3301, 3302.
entries.content: AUTHORITY: 5 U.S.C. 3301, 3302.

entries.title:
SOURCE: 28 FR 10023, Sept. 14, 1963, unless
otherw...
entries.content:
SOURCE: 28 FR 10023, Sept. 14, 1963, unless
otherwise noted.

entries.title:
$2.1 Competitive examinations and el-
igible regis...
entries.content:
$2.1 Competitive examinations and el-
igible registers; suitability and fit-
ness for civil service employment.

entries.title: (a) OPM shall be responsible for:
entries.content: (a) OPM shall be responsible for:

entries.title:
(i) Open competitive examinations
for admission to...
entries.content:
(i) Open competitive examinations
for admission to the competitive serv-
ice that will fairly test the relative ca-
pacity and fitness of the persons exam-
ined for the position to be filled.

entries.title:
(ii) Standards with respect to citizen-
ship, age,...
entries.content:
(ii) Standards with respect to citizen-
ship, age, education, training and expe-
rience, physical and mental fitness,
and for residence or other requirements
that applicants must meet to be admit-
ted to or rated in examinations.

entries.title:
(iii) Standards of suitability based on
character ...
entries.content:
(iii) Standards of suitability based on
character and conduct for appointment
to a position in the competitive serv-
ice, for appointment to a position in
the excepted service where the incum-
bent can be noncompetitively con-
verted to the competitive service, and
for career appointment to a position in
the Senior Executive Service.

entries.title:
(iv) Minimum standards of fitness
based on charact...
entries.content:
(iv) Minimum standards of fitness
based on character and conduct for ap-
pointment in any other position in the
excepted service of the executive
branch, except for (A) positions in any
element of the intelligence community
as defined in the National Security Act
of 1947, as amended, to the extent they
are not otherwise subject to OPM ap-
pointing authorities, and (B) positions

entries.title: 5 CFR Ch. | (1-1-22 Edition)
entries.content: 5 CFR Ch. | (1-1-22 Edition)

entries.title:
where OPM is statutorily precluded
from prescribin...
entries.content:
where OPM is statutorily precluded
from prescribing such standards.”

entries.title:
(b) In addition to the names of per-
sons who qual...
entries.content:
(b) In addition to the names of per-
sons who qualify in competitive exami-
nations, the names of persons who have
lost eligibility on a career or career-
conditional register because of service
in the armed forces, and the names of
persons who lost opportunity for cer-
tification or who have served under ca-
reer or career-conditional appointment
when OPM determines that they should
be given certification, may also be en-
tered at such places on appropriate reg-
isters and under such conditions as
OPM may prescribe.

entries.title:
(c) Whenever the Office of Personnel
Management (1...
entries.content:
(c) Whenever the Office of Personnel
Management (1) is unable to certify a
sufficient number of names to permit
the appointing officer to consider three
eligibles for appointment to a fourth-
class postmaster position in accord-
ance with the regular procedure, or (2)
finds that a particular rate of com-
pensation for fourth-class postmaster
positions is too low to warrant regular
competitive examinations for such po-
sitions, it may authorize appointment
to any such position or positions in ac-
cordance with such procedure as may
be prescribed by OPM. Persons ap-
pointed under this paragraph may ac-
quire competitive status subject to sat-
isfactory completion of a probationary
period prescribed by OPM.

entries.title: §2.2 Appointments.
entries.content: §2.2 Appointments.

entries.title:
(a) OPM shall establish and admin-
ister a career-...
entries.content:
(a) OPM shall establish and admin-
ister a career-conditional appointment
system for positions subject to com-
petitive examinations which will per-
mit adjustment of the career service to
necessary fluctuations in Federal em-
ployment, and provide an equitable and
orderly system for stabilizing the Fed-
eral work force. A competitive status
shall be acquired by a career-condi-
tional appointee upon satisfactory
completion of a probationary period,
but the appointee shall have career-
conditional tenure for a period of serv-
ice to be prescribed by regulation of
OPM. When an employee has completed
